rulings.Case Title: EDWARD LOPEZ et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 18, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. DEFENDANT AURORA PUMP CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Lopez worked with or around any Aurora pumps. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD LOPEZ et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD LOPEZ et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 18, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. DEFENDANT TECUMSEH PRODUCTS COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Lopez ever worked with or around any Tecumseh semi-hermetic compressors, and even if he did, whether he was exposed to any asbestos-containing parts or materials attributable to defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA BARNES et al VS. AC AND S, INC., et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 18, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 2. Defendant CertainTeed Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication on punitive damages is granted. Spain and Puerto Rico¿s laws control on the issue of availability of punitive damage s because the alleged exposures to defendant¿s asbestos cement pipe occurred in Spain and Puerto Rico. California¿s interest, if any, in having its punitive damages laws applied to this case as to defendant is very attenuated and must give way to the more substantial interest of Spain and Puerto Rico in having their laws apply to exposures that occurred in their jurisdictions. Plaintiffs have previously notified the court that they intend to contest this tentative ruling. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 18, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 5, DEFENDANT UNITED TECHNOLOGIES CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The statement that ¿plaintiffs are unable to admit or deny¿ RFA 57 satisfies defendant¿s burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to prove causation for each of their claims. Plaintiffs did not present any evidence creating a triable fact as to causation. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY M PARKER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 18, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 6. DEFENDANT J.C. PENNEY CORPORATION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products that he purchased from a J.C. Penny store. Defendant¿s D¿Amico objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)
